title: Settings: Tracing
text: Settings: Tracing Choose the level or type of ProcScript-related tracing to be displayed in the message frame or transcript window. The location where messages are displayed is determined by the settings in the Message Frame tab. ProcScript error numbers -59, -65, -1109, -1121, -1122, and -1123 are always logged regardless of the tracing options that are set. Fields Trace Print Level —the type of ProcScript-related information to be displayed Validation —steps in the Uniface validation processes. Triggers — triggers that are activated and exited. Triggers are identified by their name and the name of their associated component, entity, or field. ACT indicates that the trigger is activated, END that it is exited. Transient ProcScript errors — $procerror and $procerrorcontext information for ProcScript errors that do not change the value of $status ; for example, when an expression contains a function that fails. ProcScript errors — $procerror and $procerrorcontext information for each ProcScript error in which the execution of the ProcScript statement fails. These types of ProcScript errors set $status to a negative number. Activate errors — $procerror and $procerrorcontext information for the activate-related ProcScript errors: -50 , -51 , and -58 . ProcScript tracing —all ProcScript statements that are executed You can more easily view the context of ProcScript errors by choosing View > Error Context . Related Links Settings: I/O Print Choose the level of I/O messages that are displayed in the message frame or transcript window. Settings: Triggers Choose the triggers you want to be enabled or disabled for debugging, or those that you want to be set as breakpoints. Settings: Errors Set a specific ProcScript error value ($procerror) as a breakpoint. Settings: Message Frame Specify whether the clrmess ProcScript instruction is enabled or disabled, and whether application messages are sent to the application's message frame or to the application's transcript window. Error Context Displays the contents of $procerrorcontext whenever a ProcScript error occurs ($procerror< 0). Return Values, Status Values, and ProcScript Errors ProcScript modules return a numeric status value in $status. This module may contain the value given as an argument to the return or exit statement, a value that indicates successful completion of a command or module, or a negative number that reflects an error. <$triggerAbbr> The trigger name abbreviation.
